documentation.value:
Creates a new resource group.
```bash
az group create --name myRG --location eastus
az group create --name myRG --location westus2 --tags env=dev
```
Name of the new resource group.
Azure region (e.g. `eastus`, `westus2`, `northeurope`).
Optional space-separated tags in `key=value` format.

Creates a new virtual machine.
```bash
az vm create --resource-group myRG --name myVM --image Ubuntu2204 --size Standard_B1s --admin-username azureuser --generate-ssh-keys
```
Name of the resource group.
Name of the virtual machine.
OS image URN or alias (e.g. `Ubuntu2204`, `Win2019Datacenter`).
Optional VM size (e.g. `Standard_B1s`, `Standard_D2s_v3`).
Administrator username for the VM.
Generate SSH public and private key files if they don't exist.

Creates a new storage account.
```bash
az storage account create --name mystorageacct --resource-group myRG --location eastus --sku Standard_LRS
```
Globally unique storage account name (3-24 lowercase letters/digits).
Name of the resource group.
Azure region.
Storage SKU: `Standard_LRS`, `Standard_GRS`, `Standard_ZRS`, `Premium_LRS`.

Creates a new web app.
```bash
az webapp create --resource-group myRG --plan myPlan --name myApp --runtime "NODE:18-lts"
```
Name of the resource group.
Name of the App Service plan.
Globally unique name for the web app.
Optional runtime stack (e.g. `"NODE:18-lts"`, `"PYTHON:3.11"`).

Creates a new Azure Key Vault.
```bash
az keyvault create --name myVault --resource-group myRG --location eastus
```
Globally unique name for the key vault.
Name of the resource group.
Azure region.
Optional SKU: `standard` (default) or `premium`.

Sets the current active subscription.
```bash
az account set --subscription "My Subscription"
az account set --subscription 12345678-abcd-efgh-ijkl-123456789012
```
Subscription name or ID to set as active.

Logs in to Azure.
```bash
az login
az login --use-device-code
az login --tenant myorg.onmicrosoft.com
```
Use device code flow for environments without a browser.
Optional Azure AD tenant domain or ID.

Logs out and removes access to Azure subscriptions.
```bash
az logout
az logout --username user@example.com
```
Optional specific account to log out.

Manages Azure CLI configuration including default values.
```bash
az configure --defaults group=myRG location=eastus
az configure --defaults web=myApp
```
Space-separated defaults in `key=value` format (e.g. `group=myRG`).
Scope of defaults: `global` (default) or `local`.
